topic: Someone finds their college application essay published as an example
raw_output:
[story 1]

Elara scrolled through the digital archive, a half-finished project from a former classmate. Then, there it was: her meticulously crafted personal statement, perfectly formatted, nestled between two other submissions. A slow, involuntary smile stretched across her face, pulling at the corners of her mouth. She leaned closer to the screen, her breath catching slightly. The essay described her struggle with abstract mathematics, framing it as a journey into the chaotic beauty of unsolved theorems. She reread the opening paragraph, a soft chuckle escaping her throat. "Oh, that's rich," she murmured to the empty room, tapping the screen lightly with a fingertip. The sheer audacity of it—her own intellectual turmoil, distilled into polished prose—made her want to laugh out loud.

[story 2]

Mark found the file tucked deep within a shared drive, labeled simply, "Sample Prompts." He clicked it open, expecting a boilerplate template, but what greeted him was his own narrative, polished to an almost unnerving degree. The essay detailed a disastrous attempt at baking sourdough bread, using the flour dust and the resulting culinary failure as a metaphor for unexpected life pivots. Mark leaned back in his chair, his shoulders shaking with suppressed mirth. He let out a short, sharp exhale, a sound halfway between a snort and a suppressed laugh. He traced the lines of the carefully constructed narrative with his eyes, shaking his head slowly. It was absurdly self-aware, and he couldn't stop the small, rhythmic twitch of his lips.

[story 3]

When Chloe opened the PDF, a wave of unexpected lightness washed over her. It was her essay, submitted years ago for an internship application, now featured prominently on a university admissions page as an exemplar of 'vulnerability in professional settings.' She squinted at the description—her carefully worded confession about procrastinating on a major project—and a genuine, unrestrained grin spread across her face. Chloe covered her mouth with one hand, muffling a soft, bubbling sound. She let her hand drop, the tension draining from her posture. She looked around the quiet apartment, feeling a sudden, buoyant energy. This was… unexpected. She let out a soft, breathy laugh, the sound airy and unforced.
